title: The Science Behind Alcohol Cravings
description: Long-form YouTube explainer on the neuroscience of alcohol cravings: dopamine, conditioned cues, and evidence-based strategies (urge surfing, HALT, NAC, naltrexone) to manage them. No moralising.
hook: Your brain isn't broken. Here's what's actually happening.
keyMessage: Cravings are predictable neurochemistry, not personal failure.

cta.text: Subscribe for the rest of the neuroscience series

strategy.keyMessage: Cravings are predictable neurochemistry, not personal failure.
strategy.ctaText: Subscribe for the rest of the neuroscience series

creativePlan.hook: Your brain isn't broken. Here's what's actually happening.
creativePlan.musicNotes: Sparse synth bed; silence under research overlays

compliance.disclosureCopy: Educational content. Not medical advice. Talk to a clinician about pharmacotherapy.

videoLong.hook: If you've ever felt like a 4 p.m. craving was bigger than your willpower — your brain isn't broken. In the next 14 minutes I'll walk you through what's actually happening neurologically, and three evidence-based tools that change the math.

sequenceBlocks.description: Cold open: timestamp on a clock reading 4:00. VO establishes the universal experience. State the promise: mechanism + 3 tools.
Section 1: Dopamine + conditioned cues. Animation of the reward loop. Lower-third citations on every claim.
Section 2: Tool #1 — Urge surfing. Walk through the technique on-camera with a stopwatch.
Section 3: Tool #2 — HALT framework. Define each letter; show real diary examples on screen.
Section 4: Tool #3 — Pharmacotherapy primer (NAC, naltrexone). Heavy disclaimer copy on screen. Frame as 'talk to a clinician,' not advice.
Recap the three tools as bulletpoints; subscribe pitch tied to the next episode (relapse psychology).

videoLong.voiceoverNotes: Calm, neutral, low register. No 'YOU CAN DO IT' energy. Read the disclaimers slowly and at full volume — they're a feature, not a footnote.
